task_description: Write a method in Dafny to sort DNA bases (A, C, G, T) in order using a four-way partitioning algorithm similar to the Dutch National Flag problem.

repair_strategy.problem_summary: Missing loop invariants cause index out of range errors, precondition failures, and postcondition verification failures in a four-way partitioning algorithm.

problem_details.what: The while loop in the Sorter method lacks invariants to describe: (1) the ordering relationship between partition pointers c, next, g, t, (2) the sequence length preservation, (3) the multiset preservation, and (4) the element types in each partition region. This causes multiple errors: 'index out of range' when accessing sobases[next], precondition failures when calling Exchanger, and inability to prove the postconditions bordered(sobases) and multiset preservation.
problem_details.why: This is a four-way partitioning algorithm (similar to Dutch National Flag but with 4 colors: A, C, G, T). The algorithm maintains four regions: [0,c) for A, [c,next) for C, [g,t) for G, and [t,|bases|) for T. The region [next,g) contains unprocessed elements. Without the invariant '0 <= c <= next <= g <= t <= |bases|', Dafny cannot verify that 'next' is a valid index (next < g implies next < |bases|). Without region-specific invariants, Dafny cannot prove the final sorted property.
problem_details.how: Add seven loop invariants: (1) 'invariant 0 <= c <= next <= g <= t <= |bases|' to establish pointer ordering and prove index safety, (2) 'invariant |sobases| == |bases|' to preserve length, (3) 'invariant multiset(bases) == multiset(sobases)' to preserve elements, (4-7) Four invariants for each region: 'forall i :: 0 <= i < c ==> sobases[i] == A', 'forall i :: c <= i < next ==> sobases[i] == C', 'forall i :: g <= i < t ==> sobases[i] == G', 'forall i :: t <= i < |bases| ==> sobases[i] == T'. When next == g, the unprocessed region is empty, and these invariants directly imply bordered(sobases).
